description_long: This is a compact hand axe featuring a smoothly contoured wooden handle with a natural grain pattern and a slight ergonomic curve for improved grip. The handle has a rounded end with a hole, likely for hanging or attaching a lanyard. The axe head is metallic, with a broad, flat blade and a rectangular poll opposite the cutting edge. The blade is single-edged and extends outward in a straight line, while the poll is thick and flat, suitable for hammering. The head is mounted securely onto the handle, with the wood fitting snugly into the eye of the blade. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative markings or branding.
description: A compact hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, single-edged metal blade. The design is functional and sturdy, with a natural wood finish and a plain metallic head.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved wooden handle and the full profile of the broad, flat metal blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the rectangular poll, the thickness of the blade, and the snug fit of the handle into the axe head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the straight cutting edge of the blade.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the handle's end hole and the secure mounting of the blade onto the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with ergonomic grip, rounded end, visible grain, and a lanyard hole; oval cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, broad, flat steel blade with a straight edge and rectangular poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle fitted through the eye of the blade.
